abstract:
This paper introduces **CodeT5+**, a new family of encoder-decoder open large language models (LLMs) designed to overcome the architectural rigidities and objective-specific limitations of existing code models. While many models are optimized for either understanding or generation, CodeT5+ utilizes a flexible modular design that allows it to operate in encoder-only, decoder-only, or encoder-decoder modes depending on the downstream task. This versatility is supported by a diverse mixture of pretraining objectives—including span denoising, causal language modeling, contrastive learning, and text-code matching—applied to both unimodal code and bimodal text-code datasets. 

To achieve scalability, the authors propose a compute-efficient training strategy that initializes the model with frozen, off-the-shelf LLMs, and they further explore instruction tuning to improve natural language alignment. Evaluated across more than 20 code intelligence benchmarks, CodeT5+ achieves state-of-the-art results in various settings, including zero-shot and retrieval-augmented generation. Notably, the instruction-tuned 16B version sets a new performance benchmark for open-source models on the HumanEval task, achieving 35.0% pass@1 and surpassing OpenAI’s code-cushman-001. All models and code are made publicly available to support the research community.
